jd_text: Software engineer position at a tech company. Requires Python and React.
report:
Running startup checks and reading the evaluation mode format so the report matches career-ops blocks A–G.
Running startup checks and reading the evaluation mode format so the report matches career-ops blocks A–G.
# Evaluation: Software Engineer — Unknown Company

**Input type:** JD text only (no URL, no company name) — liveness cannot be verified.  
**Archetype:** General full-stack / platform engineer (outside the 6 AI archetypes; no agentic, LLMOps, or PM signals in the JD).

---

## Block A — Role Summary

| Field | Value |
|-------|--------|
| **Archetype** | General full-stack (not AI-specialized) |
| **Domain** | Unknown — “tech company” only |
| **Function** | Build (inferred from “software engineer”) |
| **Seniority** | Unspecified |
| **Remote** | Unspecified |
| **Team size** | Unspecified |
| **TL;DR** | Generic software engineer role requiring Python and React, with no scope, company, location, or seniority stated. |

---

## Block B — Match with CV

| JD requirement | CV evidence | Match |
|----------------|-------------|-------|
| **Python** | Skills: “Python (FastAPI)”; Career Ops Dashboard: “FastAPI backend”; autonomous agent infra on Linux VPS | **Partial** — demonstrated in side projects, not primary day job |
| **React** | Skills: “React 19”; Spotshop Transporter PWA: “React 19 + Vite + Tailwind CSS”; Plugly AI Chat widget work | **Strong** — production-shipped and current stack |
| **Software engineer (general)** | 8+ years frontend; Bewise (2021–2026); Various (2018–2021); full-stack side projects | **Strong** on shipping; **unclear** on backend-heavy expectations |

### Gaps

| Gap | Severity | Mitigation |
|-----|----------|------------|
| **Python as primary language** | Possible blocker if role is backend-heavy | Lead with Career Ops (Astro + **FastAPI** + PostgreSQL) and agent infra; clarify in application whether Python is UI-adjacent (APIs, tooling) or core backend ownership |
| **No domain/scope** | High uncertainty | Ask for team, product, and first 90-day expectations before investing heavily |
| **Title vs. identity** | Soft | CV positions you as “AI Frontend Engineer”; reframe as “full-stack-capable frontend engineer with production React and working Python” |
| **Company/location/remote** | Blocker until clarified | Denmark-based (Hjørring); confirm remote/hybrid and timezone before applying |

**Net:** React is a clear fit. Python is present but secondary relative to your e-commerce/frontend career arc.

---

## Block C — Level & Strategy

| Dimension | Assessment |
|-----------|------------|
| **JD level** | Unspecified — could be mid or senior |
| **Your natural level** | Mid–senior frontend; junior–mid on Python-as-primary-backend |
| **Positioning** | “8 years shipping production UIs; extending into full-stack with FastAPI backends I deploy myself (nginx, Docker, Tailscale)” |

**Sell senior without overstating:**
- Spotshop Transporter PWA — you owned the full frontend on a production driver workflow.
- Career Ops Dashboard — end-to-end product (10 pages, FastAPI, deployment).
- Plugly AI Chat — LLM integration in a real e-commerce context.

**If they downlevel:** Only worth it if comp reflects frontend seniority and Python is a growth lane, not day-one backend ownership. Negotiate a 6-month scope review if the role turns backend-heavy.

---

## Block D — Comp & Demand

| Factor | Finding |
|--------|---------|
| **Company-specific comp** | Not assessable — company unknown |
| **Role market (Denmark, React + Python)** | Full-stack roles with React are common; Python + React pairs appear in startups and product teams. No JD-level or location to anchor a range. |
| **Demand** | React remains high demand; Python adds full-stack breadth. Generic postings are hard to prioritize. |
| **Your target title** | Profile: “Frontend Engineer / AI Frontend Engineer” — this role may not align with AI-frontend positioning unless the team builds AI products. |

**Recommendation:** Do not benchmark comp on this posting alone. If you get a company name and location, rerun comp research (Glassdoor, Jobindex, Levels.fyi for larger firms).

---

## Block E — Customization Plan

| # | Section | Current | Proposed change | Why |
|---|---------|---------|-----------------|-----|
| 1 | **Headline** | AI Frontend Engineer | “Frontend Engineer · React · Python (FastAPI)” for this application | Mirrors JD keywords without hiding AI work |
| 2 | **Summary** | AI-native emphasis first | Add one line: “Production React across PWAs and widgets; Python/FastAPI for backends I own end-to-end.” | Balances React strength with Python proof |
| 3 | **Skills order** | Frontend first | Move “Python (FastAPI)” directly under React in the skills block | JD lists both equally |
| 4 | **Experience — Career Ops** | Under Side Projects | Elevate to prominent bullet under Bewise or a “Selected full-stack work” callout | Only cited FastAPI production artifact |
| 5 | **De-emphasize** | DanDomain/Hostedshop depth | Keep but shorten for non-e-commerce employers | Reduces “niche platform” misread unless they’re commerce |

**Top 5 CV tweaks:** headline, summary sentence, skills ordering, Career Ops prominence, one FastAPI architecture bullet (routing, PostgreSQL, deployment — no invented metrics).

**Top 5 LinkedIn tweaks:** same headline; featured link to Career Ops or GitHub; skills endorsements for React + Python; About paragraph mirroring summary; experience bullet for Spotshop PWA.

---

## Block F — Interview Plan

| # | JD requirement | STAR+R story | S | T | A | R | Reflection |
|---|----------------|--------------|---|---|---|---|------------|
| 1 | React | Spotshop Transporter PWA | Driver pickup/delivery workflow needed a reliable mobile UI | Build driver-facing PWA under agency constraints | React 19 + Vite + Tailwind; integrated with order API via C# backend | Shipped production PWA for real drivers | Would document API contract earlier with backend dev |
| 2 | React (widgets) | Bewise Plus widget suite | Multiple DanDomain shops needed custom UI | Maintain widgets across live SMB stores | Smarty + JsRender, JSON API, 500K+ catalogs | Widgets deployed across production webshops | Platform quirks (pricing, search) taught defensive coding |
| 3 | Python | Career Ops Dashboard | Personal job-search pipeline needed a real backend | Full-stack dashboard with AI evaluation | Astro frontend, **FastAPI** backend, PostgreSQL, nginx + Tailscale deploy | 10 functional pages, multi-provider AI routing | Validates you can own Python services, not just scripts |
| 4 | Python + integration | Plugly AI Chat | Shops needed shop-aware AI assistant | Zero-config widget on any DanDomain store | DOM context loading, `/json/products`, Cloudflare Worker → OpenRouter | Shop-agnostic assistant in development | Shows API integration pattern beyond React-only |
| 5 | Software engineer (shipping) | Agency e-commerce (2018–2021) | Danish SMB clients, tight deadlines | Deliver accessible production webshops | Responsive frontends on DanDomain | Real orders for real businesses | Agency pace = strong delivery discipline |
| 6 | Full-stack ownership | Autonomous agent infra | Needed persistent agent on VPS | Run production AI tooling yourself | systemd, multi-model routing, git-tracked knowledge vault | Live infra you operate | Demonstrates ops maturity for small teams |

**Recommended case study:** Career Ops Dashboard — full-stack, FastAPI, deployment, AI integration; maps directly to “Python and React” even though the UI is Astro.

**Red-flag questions:**
- *“Is this a frontend-heavy or backend-heavy role?”* → Prefer frontend-led full-stack; clarify Python scope.
- *“Why e-commerce background?”* → Production delivery at scale, API integration, real users — transferable to any product UI.
- *“How much Python have you done professionally?”* → Honest: primary career is frontend; FastAPI backends and agent tooling are real, recent, and self-deployed.

---

## Block G — Posting Legitimacy

**Assessment tier:** **Proceed with Caution**

| Signal | Finding | Weight |
|--------|---------|--------|
| Posting freshness | No URL — cannot verify | Neutral |
| Apply path | Unknown | Neutral |
| Description quality | Extremely generic; only Python + React named | **Concerning** |
| Company identity | “Unknown” / “tech company” | **Concerning** |
| Requirements realism | Too little text to judge contradictions | Neutral |
| Layoffs / hiring freeze | No company to search | N/A |
| Reposting | No company in scan history | N/A |
| Role–company fit | Cannot assess | Neutral |

**Context notes:** This may be a placeholder, an internal draft, or a recruiter teaser. A legitimate early-stage posting can be vague, but **without company name, URL, location, or seniority, you cannot confirm it is a real opening.** Treat as **unverified** until you have a link or named employer.

**Verification:** Cannot run `check-liveness.mjs` — no URL provided.

---

## Summary

| Dimension | Verdict |
|-----------|---------|
| **React** | Strong match |
| **Python** | Partial — proven in projects, not primary employment history |
| **Role fit** | Unclear — generic JD vs. your AI-frontend / e-commerce positioning |
| **Action** | **Do not apply on this text alone.** Request company name, posting URL, location/remote policy, and whether Python is primary or supporting. If it’s React-first full-stack with Python for APIs/tooling, worth a closer look (~3.5+). If backend-heavy Python, likely a stretch. |

**Score:** **3.2/5**  
**Legitimacy:** **Proceed with Caution**

---

legitimacy.reasoning: The description is extremely vague — no company name, location, salary, responsibilities, or specific requirements beyond two common technologies. Legitimate postings typically include far more detail.
